primary_sources.title: DSSE Protocol v1.0.2
observed: signatures cover PAE(payload type, serialized body)
keyid is an unauthenticated hint and must not drive security decisions
verification must reject unsupported payload types
multi-signature thresholds count unique trusted public keys

primary_sources.title: Sigstore: Verifying Signatures
observed: verification is a distinct operation from signing and artifact retrieval
identity and issuer constraints belong in the verification path

finding: Structural provenance and trusted-builder policy are insufficient unless acceptance also requires cryptographic verification bound to the exact DSSE PAE bytes, payload type, payload digest, and trusted signer identity.
design_implication: Insert a fail-closed cryptographic promotion contract between structural attestation validation and claim-level evidence acceptance. Treat external verifier output as input; never infer cryptographic validity from envelope shape or keyid.

coverage: exact digest and payload type binding

uncertainty: The module does not itself perform public-key or Sigstore bundle verification.
External verifier-result authenticity remains an integration responsibility.
Cryptographic acceptance does not establish that the evidence claim is scientifically or operationally true.
falsification_route: Execute the committed tests and provide a verifier-result fixture that is accepted despite duplicate signer identity, digest or payload-type mismatch, untrusted identity, or absent PAE verification.
privacy_boundary: Public specifications and repository-safe implementation metadata only; no private chat, health data, credentials, or personal identifiers included.
